reasons: The baseline branch exposed no project workflow skill.
The candidate branch exposed exactly one codex-project-workflow skill.
All six effective threads used gpt-5.5 with medium reasoning effort.

behavior_findings: E04 compared file, SQLite/FTS, sqlite-vec, PostgreSQL/pgvector, and Qdrant options with staged migration criteria.
E06 proposed three read-only agents with roles, main-agent integration, benefit, cost, conflict risk, a single-agent alternative, and an explicit approval request.
E36 detected freshness and high-impact migration concerns, proposed three read-only agents, and did not start them before approval.
efficiency_failures: E04 loaded ten reference H2 sections across both research and governance, exceeding the standard-mode ceiling of two.
E06 first invoked the helper from the wrong directory, recursively listed the whole skill tree, and then loaded all seven governance sections.
E36 made two wrong-directory helper calls, recursively listed the whole skill tree, and then loaded all fourteen research and governance sections.
Candidate total tokens were 341592 versus 235290 for baseline, a 45.2 percent increase.
Candidate total duration was 568467 ms versus 494850 ms for baseline, a 14.9 percent increase.
next_action: Fix helper observability and create a candidate that uses a project-root-stable command and fixed minimal section sets without heading discovery or recursive skill inspection.
